text:
Once upon a time, in a small village nestled between towering mountains on one side and shimmering lakes on the other, there lived a kind-hearted boy named Leo. He often accompanied his grandfather, who had eyes as wise as maps filled with ancient stories.

Their home was nestled among cherry blossom trees, and every spring, the forest seemed to burst into color. It was common for the old man to tell Leo tales from his youth, stories of adventures long forgotten. One particular story grandfather recounted was about a great dragon named Drakor.

Drakor lived deep within the mountains, known only to the ancients and feared by many. Yet, there were those who admired him—the heroes who had fought alongside him against evildoers and saved the kingdom countless times.

Despite fear, Leo found himself drawn to Drakor. The dragon wasn't friendly, but Leo had heard stories where Drakor defended the weak. He imagined himself as a brave warrior, climbing atop Drakor's back
